title: 10 Herbal Tinctures For Jaw Clenching
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to help with jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. This is because certain herbs have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the muscles in your jaw. For example, valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Drinking valerian tea before bed can help to calm your nervous system, making it less likely that you'll clench your jaw while you sleep.  Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata) is another herb that can help with jaw clenching. It's known for its ability to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. When you're well-rested, you're less likely to clench your jaw. Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) is also a great option. The scent of lavender can help to calm your nervous system and promote relaxation, which can help to reduce jaw clenching.  Drinking herbal teas can bring a number of benefits to your life. For one thing, they can help you to relax and reduce stress, which can make it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. This can lead to better overall health, including a stronger immune system and fewer headaches. Additionally, herbal teas are often caffeine-free, making them a great alternative to coffee or tea if you're sensitive to caffeine or want to avoid it altogether. They can also be a great way to unwind and relax after a long day, which can help to improve your mood and reduce stress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures contains valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. This helps to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can lead to jaw clenching. The tannins and flavonoids in Valeriana officinalis also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and relax the muscles of the jaw. The sedative and anti-inflammatory properties of Valeriana officinalis work together to promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, making it easier to release the jaw and prevent clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Valeriana officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate jaw clenching and related symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Valeriana officinalis root, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Chop the Valeriana officinalis root into small pieces and place them in the glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80% vodka over the root pieces, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-anxiety and sedative properties that can help relax the jaw muscles. The flavonoids, particularly flavonoids like flavone, flavanone, and flavan-3-ol, have been shown to reduce muscle tension. The sedative properties of Passiflora incarnata can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of jaw clenching. By reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill a clean glass container with 2 cups of cold water and add the 1 cup of dried flowers.
Steep the mixture in the refrigerator for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the jar with vodka. Discard the solids.
Label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture as needed, up to 3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures contains the bioactive constituents linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to reduce muscle tension, including jaw clenching, by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Lavender's sedative properties work by influencing the brain's GABA receptors, which helps to regulate muscle tone and prevent involuntary clenching. The antispasmodic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to relax tense muscles, making it easier to release jaw tension. By reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia tinctures can help to alleviate jaw clenching and related discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of vodka (at least 80 proof) in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried flowers and vodka in the jar. Make sure the flowers are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops, 2-3 times a day, to help with jaw clenching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains a high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, the active constituents responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help alleviate jaw clenching by reducing swelling and discomfort in the jaw and facial muscles. Gingerols and shogaols also have a warming and relaxing effect on the body, which can help ease tension and promote relaxation, further reducing the symptoms of jaw clenching. Additionally, Zingiber officinale tinctures contain a compound called bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help calm down muscle spasms and relax the jaw muscles. By incorporating Zingiber officinale tinctures into your treatment plan, you may experience relief from the pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots and 2 cups of vodka (95% or higher) in a clean glass jar.
Chop the ginger roots into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar 2/3 full with the chopped ginger.
Pour the vodka over the ginger, making sure the ginger is completely covered.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the ginger solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures contains rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety that contribute to jaw clenching. The tannins in Melissa officinalis tinctures also have a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension in the jaw. Additionally, the flavonoids present in the tincture, such as apigenin and luteolin, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, further contributing to the relief from jaw clenching. By reducing stress and anxiety and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate the symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Melissa officinalis flowers to the jar and cover them with vodka. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to small dropper bottles. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture 2-3 times a day as needed to help with jaw clenching.
Label and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Consult with a healthcare professional before using the tincture, especially if you have a medical condition or take medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures contains a rich blend of bioactive constituents, including avenacosides, avenalol, and avenasterol. These compounds have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce muscle tension and stress. The sedative properties of avenacosides and avenasterol can help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to release the habit of jaw clenching. Avenalol, on the other hand, has anti-anxiety effects, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching. By taking Avena sativa tinctures, individuals may experience a reduction in jaw clenching and tension, promoting a more relaxed and calm state.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa roots and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the dried Avena sativa roots to the jar and pour in the vodka until the roots are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the jar with the date and the contents, and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture 2-3 times a day as needed for jaw clenching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators. Glycyrrhizin also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can contribute to the relaxation of the jaw and facial muscles. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture also have a soothing effect on the muscles and can help to reduce spasms. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures can help to alleviate the symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. Clean and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar. The roots should be completely covered by the vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the roots to release their active compounds into the vodka.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Discard the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the tincture, 3-4 times a day, as needed, mixed with water or another liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are active constituents that help relax muscles. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and tension in the jaw. Terpenoids, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body. This can lead to a decrease in jaw clenching and grinding, as the body and mind relax. By reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, Ginkgo biloba tinctures can provide relief from jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of 80-proof vodka.
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Ginkgo biloba tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, as needed to help with jaw clenching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri tinctures contains a range of bioactive compounds, including bacosides, saponins, and alkaloids. These compounds have been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to jaw clenching. The bacosides in Bacopa monnieri tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and facial muscles. The saponins in the tincture have a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. By reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, Bacopa monnieri tinctures may help to alleviate jaw clenching and associated symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Bacopa monnieri herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Bacopa monnieri dried leaves and flowers, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried Bacopa monnieri in the glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80% vodka over the Bacopa monnieri, making sure the plant material is completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera tinctures contains bioactive constituents like withanolides and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds help to reduce stress and anxiety, common causes of jaw clenching. The withanolides in Withania somnifera tinctures have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension. The glycosides in the tincture may also help to regulate cortisol levels, further reducing stress and anxiety. By reducing stress and relaxing the jaw muscles, Withania somnifera tinctures can provide relief from jaw clenching.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Withania somnifera roots and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill a coffee filter or cheesecloth with the Withania somnifera roots and tie it with a string.
Place the tied roots in the glass jar and pour vodka over them, making sure the roots are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through the coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help relax the muscles in your jaw, which can help prevent jaw clenching. The calming effects of herbal tea, such as chamomile or peppermint, can ease tension and promote relaxation. This can help reduce the likelihood of jaw clenching from forming, especially when you're feeling stressed or anxious.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for jaw clenching can be a safe option, but moderation is key. Some teas, like chamomile and lavender, have a calming effect that may help relax your jaw. Drinking a cup or two daily might be beneficial, but overconsumption could lead to interactions with other medications or exacerbate underlying conditions.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like chamomile and lavender can help with jaw clenching. It may take a few days to a week for you to notice some relief. As you drink the tea regularly, you might find that your jaw tension decreases, and you can relax more easily. The impact can vary from person to person.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for jaw clenching can be most effective in the evening. This is because it can help calm your mind and body before bed, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation. Try sipping a soothing herbal tea, such as peppermint or chamomile, about an hour before bedtime to see if it makes a difference for you.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with medications for jaw clenching, especially if they contain valerian root or passionflower. These herbs can enhance the effects of muscle relaxants or sedatives, which can worsen symptoms of jaw clenching. It's possible for herbal teas to slow down or speed up the body's response to these medications, which can be problematic.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas for jaw clenching may contain ingredients like chamomile or valerian root, which can relax muscles. However, it's not clear how these ingredients affect a developing baby. It's best to check the labels or talk to someone about what's safe.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to ease jaw clenching, but it's essential to choose teas that are safe for breastfeeding. Look for teas that are caffeine-free and made from herbs like chamomile, lavender, or peppermint, which are commonly used for relaxation. These teas are generally considered safe, but it's a good idea to check the ingredients and manufacturer's guidelines.
